explanation: This LTL property states that globally, whenever the autopilot requests support (request) and the autopilot reports the correct active mode (mode), it should eventually transition to the NOMINAL state (state_is_NOMINAL). The property uses template 19 which is of the form G(bool_exp2 -> (F bool_exp3)).

The LTL property ensures that if the autopilot requests support (request) and reports the correct active mode (mode), then the sensor will eventually change its state to NOMINAL from its current state, and this will happen within a certain time limit. Specifically, the property is defined as `G ( (request & mode) -> (F[0,N_DURATION] state_is_NOMINAL) )`.  This means that whenever `request` and `mode` are simultaneously true, `state_is_NOMINAL` must eventually become true within the next `N_DURATION` time units.

chosen_LTL_template: (((((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)) V (((! bool_exp2) & ((X bool_exp2) & (! ((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1))))) -> ((X bool_exp3) & (! ((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)))))) & (bool_exp2 -> bool_exp3)) | bool_exp1)
